title: isolepis hispida
intro: Isolepis hispida, also known as the hairy club-rush, is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of mucus. In herbal preparations, Isolepis hispida is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall respiratory well-being.
